questions.question: Bellatrix Ltd. issued 2,00,000 equity shares at ₹15 (face ₹10). Terms: Application ₹5, Allotment ₹6 (incl ₹5 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 3,20,000. 20,000 rejected. Pro-rata rest. Calculate: (1) Amount of Securities Premium Reserved at allotment stage; (2) Total Securities Premium after call (if call has no premium); (3) Net amount received on allotment (bank).
options: ₹10,00,000 at allotment; ₹10,00,000 total; Bank receipt ₹7,00,000
₹8,00,000 at allotment; ₹8,00,000 total; Bank ₹4,00,000
₹5,00,000 at allotment; ₹10,00,000 total; Bank ₹7,00,000
questions.explanation: Securities Premium at allotment: 2,00,000 × ₹5 = ₹10,00,000. No premium on call (call = ₹4 all face value). Total SPR = ₹10,00,000. Valid apps = 3,00,000. Allotted = 2,00,000. Excess from valid = 1,00,000 × ₹5 = ₹5,00,000. Total allotment due = 2,00,000 × ₹6 = ₹12,00,000. Net bank receipt on allotment = ₹12,00,000 − ₹5,00,000 = ₹7,00,000. Answer A: SPR ₹10,00,000; total ₹10,00,000; bank ₹7,00,000.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): 'Called-up Capital' refers to that portion of subscribed capital which the company has demanded (called) from shareholders.
Reason (R): Called-up capital includes both the amount already paid and the amount in arrears (not yet paid by defaulting shareholders).

Choose the correct option:
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion A is TRUE — Called-up Capital is the portion of subscribed capital that has been called (demanded) by the company through application, allotment, and call notices. Reason R is also TRUE — Called-up capital includes both amounts actually paid (paid-up capital) and amounts unpaid by defaulting shareholders (calls-in-arrears). Paid-up = Called-up − Calls-in-Arrears. Both statements are true but R explains the composition of called-up capital rather than explaining why A is the definition. Hence B: Both true but R doesn't explain A.

questions.question: Mira Ltd. issued 5,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹16. Terms: Application ₹3, Allotment ₹9 (incl ₹6 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 8,00,000. Rejected 50,000. Pro-rata rest. Total refund (rejected applicants) and total excess application money adjusted:
options: Refund ₹1,50,000; Excess adjusted ₹7,50,000
Refund ₹1,50,000; Excess adjusted ₹9,00,000
Refund ₹1,00,000; Excess adjusted ₹7,50,000
Refund ₹2,00,000; Excess adjusted ₹6,50,000
questions.explanation: Refund to rejected = 50,000 × ₹3 = ₹1,50,000. Valid apps = 7,50,000. Allotted = 5,00,000. Excess from valid = 2,50,000 × ₹3 = ₹7,50,000 (adjusted against allotment). Total allotment due = 5,00,000 × ₹9 = ₹45,00,000. Excess adjusted = ₹7,50,000. Net bank on allotment = ₹45,00,000 − ₹7,50,000 = ₹37,50,000. Answer A: Refund ₹1,50,000; Excess adjusted ₹7,50,000.

questions.question: Achernar Ltd. purchased goodwill from a sole trader for ₹12,00,000. The company issued equity shares of ₹10 each at a discount of 4% in full settlement. How many shares were issued?
options: 1,25,000 shares
1,20,000 shares
1,30,000 shares
1,04,167 shares
questions.explanation: Issue price = ₹10 − 4% = ₹10 − ₹0.40 = ₹9.60 per share. Number of shares = ₹12,00,000 ÷ ₹9.60 = 1,25,000 shares. Journal: Goodwill A/c Dr ₹12,00,000 / Discount on Issue A/c Dr ₹50,000 (1,25,000 × ₹0.40) / To Share Capital A/c ₹12,50,000 (1,25,000 × ₹10). Answer A: 1,25,000 shares.

questions.question: Altair Systems Ltd. issued 4,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹17. Terms: Application ₹4, Allotment ₹9 (incl ₹7 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 6,40,000. 40,000 rejected. Pro-rata rest. Total Securities Premium Reserve credited in all entries:
questions.explanation: Total premium = ₹7 per share × 4,00,000 shares = ₹28,00,000. All premium is in allotment money (₹7 out of ₹9 allotment). Journal at allotment: Share Allotment A/c Dr ₹36,00,000 / To Share Capital A/c ₹8,00,000 (₹2 face × 4,00,000) / To Securities Premium ₹28,00,000. Total SPR = ₹28,00,000. Answer A.

questions.question:
Polaris Infra Ltd. made the following share issues:
(i) 1,00,000 equity shares at ₹15 (face ₹10) for cash
(ii) 50,000 equity shares at ₹12 (face ₹10) to vendor for land
(iii) 20,000 equity shares at ₹10 (face ₹10) as sweat equity to employees

What is the total Securities Premium Reserve from all three issues?
questions.explanation: (i) Cash issue: premium = ₹5 × 1,00,000 = ₹5,00,000. (ii) Vendor for land: premium = ₹2 × 50,000 = ₹1,00,000. (iii) Sweat equity at par: premium = ₹0. Total Securities Premium = ₹5,00,000 + ₹1,00,000 + ₹0 = ₹6,00,000. Wait — option B is ₹7,50,000: (i) ₹5,00,000 + (ii) ₹1,00,000 + additional? If sweat equity also at premium: ₹5+₹1+₹1.5 = ₹7.50. For exactly ₹6,00,000 = Option A. Answer A: ₹6,00,000. But B ₹7,50,000 requires (i) ₹5L + (ii) ₹1L + (iii) ₹1.5L — sweat equity at ₹17.50? Not stated. Answer A: ₹6,00,000.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): In a pro-rata allotment scenario, the total application money received equals the total of: (amount transferred to Share Capital) + (excess money adjusted against allotment) + (refund to rejected applicants).
Reason (R): The Share Application Account must be fully closed after allotment with a nil balance, as all application money must be accounted for.

Choose the correct option:
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are false
questions.explanation: Both A and R are TRUE and R explains A. The Share Application Account must be completely closed after allotment (nil balance), which means all application money must be categorized and transferred: (1) Amount on allotted shares → Share Capital; (2) Excess from pro-rata → adjusted against allotment (Share Allotment A/c); (3) Rejected applicants → Bank (refund). The total of these three equals total application money received. R correctly states that the nil balance requirement ensures complete accounting.

questions.question: Izar Ltd. offered 2,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 each at ₹14 per share. Application ₹4, Allotment ₹6 (incl ₹4 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 3,00,000. Pro-rata allotment. Geeta applied for 900 shares, received pro-rata, paid all till call, then paid advance of ₹1,500 for a future call. Company later made second call of ₹0 (no second call). What is the treatment of the ₹1,500 advance?
options: Forfeited by the company as no second call was made
Transferred to Securities Premium Reserve
Refunded to Geeta as the call was not made
Transferred to Capital Reserve
questions.explanation: If no second call is ever made, the Calls-in-Advance amount that was paid in anticipation of a call that never comes must be refunded to the shareholder. The company cannot retain money it has no legal basis to keep. Journal for refund: Calls-in-Advance A/c Dr ₹1,500 / To Bank A/c ₹1,500. Option C is correct — the money must be refunded. It cannot be forfeited, transferred to reserves, or kept indefinitely.

questions.question: Zubenelgenubi Ltd. took over the business of Mintaka Co. Assets: Land ₹25L, Plant ₹15L, Stock ₹8L, Debtors ₹4L. Liabilities: Creditors ₹5L, Loan ₹7L. PC = Net Assets + Goodwill 20%. Shares of ₹10 at ₹12 issued. Cash paid = ₹5,00,000. Find: (i) Net Assets; (ii) Goodwill; (iii) PC; (iv) Number of equity shares issued.
options: Net ₹40L; Goodwill ₹8L; PC ₹48L; 3,58,333 shares
Net ₹40L; Goodwill ₹8L; PC ₹48L; shares at ₹12 = 35,83,333
Net ₹40L; Goodwill ₹8L; PC ₹48L; 3,58,333 equity shares; cash ₹5L
Net ₹40L; Goodwill ₹8L; PC ₹43L; 3,16,667 shares
questions.explanation: Total Assets = 25+15+8+4 = ₹52L. Total Liabilities = 5+7 = ₹12L. Net Assets = ₹40L. Goodwill = 20% × ₹40L = ₹8L. PC = ₹48L. Cash paid = ₹5L. Shares value = ₹48L − ₹5L = ₹43L. Shares at ₹12 = ₹43,00,000 ÷ ₹12 = 3,58,333 shares. Answer C (matches answer A structure but includes the cash split). Answer C: Net ₹40L; Goodwill ₹8L; PC ₹48L; 3,58,333 shares; cash ₹5L.

questions.question: Which of the following sequences correctly shows the flow of funds in a share issue process?
options: Share Capital → Application → Allotment → Call
Bank (application money) → Share Application A/c → Share Capital A/c (on allotment) → Calls due
Securities Premium → Share Capital → Bank → Calls
Allotment → Application → Call → Share Capital
questions.explanation: The correct flow in share issue accounting is: (1) Bank A/c Dr / To Share Application A/c Cr — money received from applicants; (2) Share Application A/c Dr / To Share Capital A/c Cr + adjustments at allotment — application money transferred to capital; (3) Share Allotment A/c Dr / To Share Capital A/c Cr — allotment amount due; (4) Bank A/c Dr / To Share Allotment A/c Cr — allotment money received; (5) Share Call A/c Dr / To Share Capital A/c — call due; (6) Bank Dr / To Call A/c — call received. Option B correctly captures this flow.

questions.question: Fomalhaut Ltd. issued 1,50,000 preference shares of ₹50 at ₹65 (premium ₹15). Terms: Application ₹20 (incl ₹5 premium), Allotment ₹30 (incl ₹10 premium), Call ₹15. Applications: 2,25,000. Pro-rata. Vikram applied for 2,250 shares. He was allotted on pro-rata, paid allotment, and paid ₹22,500 as advance for call. Calculate interest on calls-in-advance @ 12% for 3 months.
questions.explanation: Calls-in-advance = ₹22,500. Interest = ₹22,500 × 12/100 × 3/12 = ₹22,500 × 0.03 = ₹675. This is paid by company to Vikram. Journal: Interest on Calls-in-Advance A/c Dr ₹675 / To Bank A/c ₹675. Answer A: ₹675.

questions.question: Mimosa Ltd. issued 1,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹18. Terms: Application ₹5, Allotment ₹9 (incl ₹8 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 1,60,000. 10,000 rejected. Pro-rata rest. Sunil applied for 750 shares. Calculate: (1) allotted shares; (2) total contribution by Sunil up to and including call stage.
options: 500 allotted; total ₹9,000
500 allotted; total ₹9,000 (consistent)
600 allotted; total ₹10,800
450 allotted; total ₹8,100
questions.explanation: Valid apps = 1,50,000. Pro-rata = 1,00,000:1,50,000 = 2:3. Sunil applied 750 → Allotted = 500. Total contribution = 500 × ₹18 = ₹9,000 (full issue price × shares allotted). Alternatively: application paid 750 × ₹5 = ₹3,750; net allotment = 500 × ₹9 − (750−500) × ₹5 = ₹4,500 − ₹1,250 = ₹3,250; call = 500 × ₹4 = ₹2,000; total = ₹3,750 + ₹3,250 + ₹2,000 = ₹9,000. Answer A: 500 allotted; total ₹9,000.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): When shares are issued at a discount to acquire assets, the asset is recorded at its agreed value (which equals shares × issue price), not at the face value of shares.
Reason (R): The agreed purchase consideration, not the face value of shares, determines the value at which assets are recorded in the buyer's books.

Choose the correct option:
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are false
questions.explanation: Both A and R are TRUE and R explains A. Assets acquired for consideration other than cash are recorded at the agreed purchase consideration = shares × issue price (which includes any discount). For example, if 1,000 shares of ₹10 at ₹9 (discount) are issued for land: Land A/c Dr ₹9,000 (agreed value = 1,000 × ₹9) / Discount on Issue Dr ₹1,000 / To Share Capital ₹10,000. The asset is at ₹9,000 (issue price), not ₹10,000 (face value). R correctly explains this principle.

questions.question: Centauri Ltd. issued 3,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹16. Terms: Application ₹4 (incl ₹1 premium), Allotment ₹8 (incl ₹5 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 5,00,000. 20,000 rejected. Pro-rata rest. Find: (a) Total bank receipts from application; (b) Total refund; (c) Total bank receipts from allotment (net); (d) Bank receipts from call.
Which of the following is NOT a method of dealing with over-subscription of shares?
options: Rejecting some applications entirely and allotting to others in full
Making pro-rata allotment to all valid applicants
Combining partial rejection and pro-rata allotment
Issuing additional shares beyond authorized capital to meet demand
questions.explanation: Option D is NOT a valid method. A company CANNOT issue shares beyond its Authorized Capital — it is the legal maximum set in the Memorandum of Association. To issue more shares beyond authorized capital, the company must first increase its authorized capital by altering its Memorandum (requiring special resolution and regulatory approval). Valid methods for handling over-subscription are: (A) full rejection of some applications, (B) pro-rata allotment to all, or (C) a combination of both.

questions.question: Serpens Ltd. issued 2,40,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹17. Terms: Application ₹5 (incl ₹2 premium), Allotment ₹8 (incl ₹5 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 3,60,000. Pro-rata. Amrita applied for 1,800 shares. She was allotted pro-rata. She paid all dues until allotment but did not pay call. Interest on calls-in-arrears @ 10% p.a. for 4 months. Total outstanding from Amrita:

Lupus Ltd. issued 50,000 equity shares at ₹15 (face ₹10). Terms: Application ₹4, Allotment ₹7 (incl ₹5 premium), Call ₹4. Applications: 90,000. 5,000 rejected. Rest pro-rata. Rajan (applied 1,700) received pro-rata allotment, paid all dues. Priya (applied 850) received pro-rata allotment, paid calls-in-advance of ₹2,000, then call was made. Priya's additional payment on call:
questions.explanation: Valid apps = 85,000. Pro-rata = 50,000:85,000 = 10:17. Priya applied 850 → Allotted = 500. Call due = 500 × ₹4 = ₹2,000. Advance = ₹2,000. Since advance = call due → Calls-in-Advance A/c Dr ₹2,000 / To Share Call A/c ₹2,000. Net additional payment = ₹0. Answer A: ₹0. But if allotted = 625 (850 × 10/17 ≈ 500): call = 500 × ₹4 = ₹2,000; advance ₹2,000 → ₹0. Answer A: ₹0. But option B ₹500 if allotted = 625 and call ₹4 × 625 = ₹2,500 − ₹2,000 advance = ₹500. Answer B if allotted = 625.

questions.question: Draco Ltd. issued equity shares and preference shares as follows: 1,00,000 equity shares of ₹10 at ₹14 and 50,000 preference shares of ₹100 at ₹110. All shares were fully subscribed and paid in a single installment. What is the total Securities Premium Reserve after both issues?
questions.explanation: Equity premium = ₹4 × 1,00,000 = ₹4,00,000. Preference premium = ₹10 × 50,000 = ₹5,00,000. Total Securities Premium Reserve = ₹4,00,000 + ₹5,00,000 = ₹9,00,000. Both equity and preference share premiums are credited to the SAME Securities Premium Reserve Account. Answer A: ₹9,00,000.

questions.question: Orion Enterprises Ltd. has the following share capital position: Authorized Capital: 5,00,000 shares of ₹10 = ₹50,00,000. Issued: 3,00,000 shares at ₹14. Subscribed: 2,80,000 shares. Called up: ₹8 per share. Paid up: ₹7.50 per share on average. Calls-in-Advance: ₹40,000. Securities Premium (₹4 per share on issued): applicable only on subscribed shares. Compute: (i) Called-up Capital; (ii) Paid-up Capital; (iii) Calls-in-Arrears.
options: Called-up ₹22,40,000; Paid-up ₹22,00,000; Arrears ₹40,000
questions.explanation: Called-up Capital = Subscribed shares × Called-up amount = 2,80,000 × ₹8 = ₹22,40,000. Paid-up per share = ₹7.50 (on average). Total Paid-up = 2,80,000 × ₹7.50 = ₹21,00,000. Calls-in-Arrears = Called-up − Paid-up = ₹22,40,000 − ₹21,00,000 = ₹1,40,000. Calls-in-Advance (₹40,000) is separately shown as current liability and does not affect paid-up calculation. Answer A: Called-up ₹22,40,000; Paid-up ₹21,00,000; Arrears ₹1,40,000.
